name: S. Martinho Route – Burgo Loop Tour
name_original: Rota de S. Martinho – Giro ad anello da Burgo
region_intro: No traffic gravel bike trails in Aveiro are characterized by the extensive Ria de Aveiro lagoon system, featuring a network of canals, salt marshes, and tidal flats. The region offers mostly flat terrain, ideal for relaxed rides, alongside coastal routes through dunes and pine forests. Inland, the Vouga Valley provides routes with more significant elevation gains. This diverse landscape offers varied surfaces for gravel biking, from smooth boardwalks to unpaved coastal trails. Best no traffic gravel bike trails in Aveiro The most popular no traffic gravel bike trail is Costa Nova – Costa Nova Striped Houses loop from Farol de Aveiro, a 13.4 miles (21.6 km) trail that takes 1 hour 19 minutes to complete. This easy route showcases the iconic striped houses and coastal scenery. Another top favourite among local gravel bikers is Ria de Aveiro Boardwalks – Ria de Aveiro Boardwalks loop from Aveiro, a moderate 21.1 miles (34.0 km) path. This route follows the scenic boardwalks along the lagoon, offering views of birdlife and marshes. Local gravel bikers also love the Paradela Station – Poço de S. Tiago Bridge loop from Cacia, a 54.7 miles (88.0 km) trail leading through varied inland terrain, often completed in about 6 hours 28 minutes. Gravel biking in Aveiro is defined by the Ria de Aveiro lagoon, coastal dunes, and pine forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy coastal paths to more challenging inland routes. The routes in Aveiro are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 100 gravel bikers have used komoot to explore Aveiro's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many no-traffic gravel bike trails are available in Aveiro?
A: Komoot offers a selection of 8 dedicated no-traffic gravel bike trails in Aveiro. These routes are designed to keep you away from vehicular traffic, providing a peaceful and immersive cycling experience through the region's unique landscapes.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly no-traffic gravel routes in Aveiro?
A: Yes, Aveiro offers easy, traffic-free gravel routes suitable for families. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23774709" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Costa Nova – Costa Nova Striped Houses loop from Farol de Aveiro&lt;/a&gt;. This route is 21.6 km long with minimal elevation gain, making it perfect for a relaxed ride to enjoy the iconic striped houses and coastal scenery.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on Aveiro's no-traffic gravel trails?
A: Aveiro's no-traffic gravel trails offer diverse scenery. You can expect to ride along the extensive &lt;strong&gt;Ria de Aveiro Lagoon&lt;/strong&gt; with its salt marshes and tidal flats, through coastal dunes and pine forests, and past picturesque beaches like Costa Nova. Some routes also venture into the Vouga Valley, offering more varied terrain and even waterfalls.

Q: Are there any challenging no-traffic gravel bike routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32145307" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paradela Station – Poço de S. Tiago Bridge loop from Cacia&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 88 km route with significant elevation gain. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2332003110" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;S. Martinho Route – Burgo Loop Tour&lt;/a&gt;, which covers nearly 25 km with over 680 meters of ascent.

Q: What are the best times of year to go gravel biking in Aveiro to avoid traffic?
A: The best times for gravel biking in Aveiro, especially for avoiding crowds and enjoying pleasant weather, are spring (April-June) and early autumn (September-October). During these periods, the weather is generally mild, and the trails are less busy than in peak summer, allowing for a more serene, traffic-free experience.

Q: Are there any loop gravel bike trails in Aveiro that are traffic-free?
A: Yes, many of Aveiro's no-traffic gravel routes are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32146814" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ria de Aveiro Boardwalks loop from Aveiro&lt;/a&gt; offers a 34 km circular ride along the lagoon, providing stunning views without needing to retrace your steps.

Q: What attractions or points of interest can I see along the no-traffic gravel bike trails?
A: Along Aveiro's no-traffic gravel trails, you can discover several points of interest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3893934" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moliceiro Boats on the Canals of Aveiro&lt;/a&gt; are a highlight in the city itself. Further afield, you might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/528323" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ovar Canal&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/573237" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barrinha de Esmoriz Nature Reserve&lt;/a&gt;, or even the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/527671" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabreia Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; on routes extending into the Vouga Valley.

Q: How long do the no-traffic gravel bike rides in Aveiro typically take?
A: The duration of no-traffic gravel bike rides in Aveiro varies significantly depending on the route's length and your pace. Shorter, easier routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23774709" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Costa Nova loop&lt;/a&gt; can take around 1.5 to 2 hours. Longer, more challenging routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32145307" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paradela Station loop&lt;/a&gt;, can extend to 6.5 hours or more.

Q: Are there any routes that combine coastal views with traffic-free gravel paths?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32147093" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Costa Nova – Mira Forest Cycle Path loop from Farol de Aveiro&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice. This moderate 49.5 km route offers a mix of coastal scenery, including the iconic Costa Nova striped houses, and peaceful paths through the Mira Forest, ensuring a largely traffic-free experience with wide ocean views.

Q: What do other gravel bikers enjoy most about the no-traffic trails in Aveiro?
A: The komoot community highly rates the no-traffic gravel trails in Aveiro, with an average score of 4.14 stars. Reviewers often praise the unique experience of cycling along the &lt;strong&gt;Ria de Aveiro Boardwalks&lt;/strong&gt;, the tranquility of the coastal pine forests, and the opportunity to explore the region's natural beauty away from busy roads. The flat terrain on many routes is also frequently highlighted as a positive for relaxed rides.
tips: Ecovia do Arda is a cycle path on the banks of the river of the same name. It is almost 11 kilometers away, stretching from Arouca to Várzea. Provides a unique immersion in the Arda Valley.
Starts off fairly gently but gets steeper and steeper with the final couple of kms sitting at 10% or above. It’s a great climb.

In charming Arouca you will find the Arouca Monastery, an architectural and historical landmark, and the picturesque streets and squares that exude ancient charm. The local gastronomy, in particular the convent sweets, is an attraction in itself. The Arouca Geopark is famous for its Paiva Walkways, offering spectacular trails and views over the Paiva River, as well as trilobite fossils, highlighting an important geological and natural heritage.
